Tests the `concat` method of the `String` object to ensure it returns an empty string when no arguments are provided.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the behavior of the `String.prototype.concat` method when called on an empty string with no arguments. The assertion verifies that the result of calling `concat` on an empty string is also an empty string. This test is narrow in scope, focusing specifically on the default behavior of the `concat` method in JavaScript when no parameters are provided.
